After that I’ll verify with the deadcode check and `go test ./...`, write the decisions log, and commit once everything is green.

cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)
- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)
- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present
- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database
- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path
- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end
- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server
- [ ] cargo test --workspace green
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.

## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)

- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug
- GraphQL under the nested path
- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404
- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths
- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST

## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)

### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced

B1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.

Wire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.

Create the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.

The legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.

### 2. Audit attribution not populated

F1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.

Find every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(&request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.

### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database

`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: &str = "x-axon-database"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:

(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR
(b) A new field on every request proto

(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `"{tenant}:{database}"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.

Also add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.

For this bead, the gRPC change is small:
- Rename the metadata constant
- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form
- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form
- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`

### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)

D2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.

## Scope summary

This bead does exactly three things:

1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)
2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present
3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata

## Tests for this bead

### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)

- create a JwtIssuer with a known key
- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)
- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses
- send a request with Authorization: Bearer <jwt> to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections
- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)

### Integration test: attribution populated

- send an entity-create request with a JWT
- query the audit log
- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub

### gRPC test: composite slug

- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`
- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)

### Manual smoke test

Start the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.

## Out of scope

- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred
- OIDC federation — follow-up
- Signing key rotation — future ADR
- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths

## Files to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)
- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup
- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path
- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW
- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW
- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it
- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal
- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes
- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes

## Verification

- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+\n+const ISSUER: &str = \"cutover-issuer\";\n+const TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\n+const DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\n+const storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n+        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n+        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n+        role: TenantRole::Write,\n+    });\n+    storage\n+}\n+\n+///     15	// HubDefaultDDxVersion is the ddx_version the hub advertises when validating
    16	// spoke handshakes. It is exposed as a field so tests and operators can pin
    17	// the hub's view of "current ddx version" without rebuilding the binary.
    18	const HubDefaultDDxVersion = "0.1.0"
    19	
    20	// federationHub holds the per-server state for hub-mode federation: the
    21	// in-memory registry, the disk path used to persist it, the warning logger
    22	// (for plain-HTTP accepted registrations), and configuration knobs.
    23	type federationHub struct {
    24		mu             sync.Mutex
    25		registry       *federation.FederationRegistry
    26		statePath      string // empty disables disk persistence
    27		allowPlainHTTP bool
    28		hubDDxVersion  string
    29		hubSchemaVer   string
    30		warnLog        func(format string, args ...any)
    31		warnings       []string // captured plain-HTTP warnings (test introspection)
    32		conflictsLog   []string // captured 409 conflict log lines (test introspection)
    33		now            func() time.Time
    34	}
    35	
    36	// EnableHubMode mounts the /api/federation/* routes on this server, switches
    37	// the hub-mode flag on, and (best effort) loads the registry from disk so a
    38	// hub restart can rebuild state from a prior session.
    39	//
    40	// Must be called before ListenAndServe / ListenAndServeTLS. Calling it more
    41	// than once is a no-op after the first call.
    42	//
    43	// allowPlainHTTP relaxes the default ts-net/loopback peer policy on the
    44	// federation routes — non-trusted peers are accepted, but each accepted
    45	// registration emits a WARN log entry. This is the operator opt-out for
    46	// federations spanning networks where ts-net is not viable.
    47	func (s *Server) EnableHubMode(allowPlainHTTP bool) {
    48		if s.hub != nil {
    49			return
    50		}
    51		statePath, err := federation.DefaultStatePath()
    52		if err != nil {
    53			statePath = ""
    54		}
    55		var reg *federation.FederationRegistry
    56		if statePath != "" {
    57			if loaded, lerr := federation.LoadStateFrom(statePath); lerr == nil {
    58				reg = loaded
    59			}
    60		}
    61		if reg == nil {
    62			reg = federation.NewRegistry()
    63		}
    64		s.hub = &federationHub{
    65			registry:       reg,
    66			statePath:      statePath,
    67			allowPlainHTTP: allowPlainHTTP,
    68			hubDDxVersion:  HubDefaultDDxVersion,
    69			hubSchemaVer:   federation.CurrentSchemaVersion,
    70			warnLog:        func(format string, args ...any) { log.Printf("WARN: "+format, args...) },
    71			now:            time.Now,
    72		}
    73		s.HubMode = true
    74	
    75		gate := func(h http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc { return s.requireFederationTrusted(h) }
    76		s.route("POST /api/federation/register", gate(s.handleFederationRegister))
    77		s.route("POST /api/federation/heartbeat", gate(s.handleFederationHeartbeat))
    78		s.route("GET /api/federation/spokes", gate(s.handleFederationListSpokes))
    79		s.route("DELETE /api/federation/spokes/{id}", gate(s.handleFederationDeregister))
    80	}
    81	
    82	// HubFederationRegistrySnapshot returns a copy of the current registry, for
    83	// tests and operator introspection.
    84	func (s *Server) HubFederationRegistrySnapshot() *federation.FederationRegistry {
    85		if s.hub == nil {
    86			return nil
    87		}
    88		s.hub.mu.Lock()
    89		defer s.hub.mu.Unlock()
    90		out := federation.NewRegistry()
    91		out.SchemaVersion = s.hub.registry.SchemaVersion
    92		out.Spokes = append(out.Spokes, s.hub.registry.Spokes...)
    93		return out
    94	}
    95	
    96	// HubFederationWarnings returns plain-HTTP-accepted-registration WARN messages
    97	// captured since the server started. Test-only helper.
    98	func (s *Server) HubFederationWarnings() []string {
    99		if s.hub == nil {
   100			return nil
   101		}
   102		s.hub.mu.Lock()
   103		defer s.hub.mu.Unlock()
   104		return append([]string(nil), s.hub.warnings...)
   105	}
   106	
   107	// HubFederationConflicts returns 409 conflict log messages captured since the
   108	// server started. Test-only helper.
   109	func (s *Server) HubFederationConflicts() []string {
   110		if s.hub == nil {
   111			return nil
   112		}
   113		s.hub.mu.Lock()
   114		defer s.hub.mu.Unlock()
   115		return append([]string(nil), s.hub.conflictsLog...)
   116	}
   117	
   118	// requireFederationTrusted enforces the federation-specific peer policy:
   119	// loopback / ts-net peers are always accepted; non-trusted peers are accepted
   120	// only when --federation-allow-plain-http is set. Distinct from
   121	// requireTrusted so the AC-strict default doesn't depend on a global flag.
   122	func (s *Server) requireFederationTrusted(next http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {
   123		return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   124			if isTrusted(r) {
   125				next(w, r)
   126				return
   127			}
   128			if s.hub != nil && s.hub.allowPlainHTTP {
   129				next(w, r)
   130				return
   131			}
   132			writeJSON(w, http.StatusForbidden, map[string]string{"error": "forbidden: ts-net or loopback peer required for /api/federation/* (use --federation-allow-plain-http to opt out)"})
   133		}
   134	}
   135	
   136	// federationRegisterRequest is the spoke→hub registration / re-registration
   137	// payload. Identity = (NodeID, IdentityFingerprint). NodeID is the stable
   138	// federation key; IdentityFingerprint distinguishes a re-registration of the
   139	// same node from a duplicate-name conflict (different machine claiming an
   140	// already-registered node_id).
   141	type federationRegisterRequest struct {
   142		NodeID               string   `json:"node_id"`
   143		IdentityFingerprint  string   `json:"identity_fingerprint,omitempty"`
   144		Name                 string   `json:"name"`
   145		URL                  string   `json:"url"`
   146		DDxVersion           string   `json:"ddx_version"`
   147		SchemaVersion        string   `json:"schema_version"`
   148		GraphQLSchemaVersion string   `json:"graphql_schema_version,omitempty"`
   149		Capabilities         []string `json:"capabilities,omitempty"`
   150	}
   151	
   152	// federationRegisterResponse is sent on a successful (200/201) registration.
   153	type federationRegisterResponse struct {
   154		NodeID string                 `json:"node_id"`
   155		Status federation.SpokeStatus `json:"status"`
   156		Reason string                 `json:"reason"`
   157		Spoke  federation.SpokeRecord `json:"spoke"`
   158	}
   159	
   160	// identityFingerprintKey is the SpokeRecord.Capabilities-adjacent slot we
   161	// stash the fingerprint in. To keep the on-disk schema stable (B14.2 froze
   162	// SpokeRecord shape), we encode it as a synthetic capability prefixed with
   163	// "@identity:". Future schema versions can hoist it to a first-class field.
   164	const identityFingerprintPrefix = "@identity:"
   165	
   166	func setIdentityFingerprint(rec *federation.SpokeRecord, fp string) {
   167		cleaned := rec.Capabilities[:0]
   168		for _, c := range rec.Capabilities {
   169			if !strings.HasPrefix(c, identityFingerprintPrefix) {
   170				cleaned = append(cleaned, c)
   171			}
   172		}
   173		rec.Capabilities = cleaned
   174		if fp != "" {
   175			rec.Capabilities = append(rec.Capabilities, identityFingerprintPrefix+fp)
   176		}
   177	}
   178	
   179	func getIdentityFingerprint(rec federation.SpokeRecord) string {
   180		for _, c := range rec.Capabilities {
   181			if strings.HasPrefix(c, identityFingerprintPrefix) {
   182				return strings.TrimPrefix(c, identityFingerprintPrefix)
   183			}
   184		}
   185		return ""
   186	}
   187	
   188	func (s *Server) handleFederationRegister(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   189		var req federationRegisterRequest
   190		if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
   191			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "invalid json: " + err.Error()})
   192			return
   193		}
   194		req.NodeID = strings.TrimSpace(req.NodeID)
   195		if req.NodeID == "" {
   196			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "node_id required"})
   197			return
   198		}
   199		if strings.TrimSpace(req.URL) == "" {
   200			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "url required"})
   201			return
   202		}
   203	
   204		hs := federation.Handshake(s.hub.hubDDxVersion, s.hub.hubSchemaVer, req.DDxVersion, req.SchemaVersion)
   205		if !hs.Accept {
   206			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{
   207				"error":  "version handshake failed",
   208				"reason": hs.Reason,
   209			})
   210			return
   211		}
   212	
   213		now := s.hub.now().UTC()
   214	
   215		s.hub.mu.Lock()
   216		defer s.hub.mu.Unlock()
   217	
   218		if existing := s.hub.registry.FindSpoke(req.NodeID); existing != nil {
   219			existingFP := getIdentityFingerprint(*existing)
   220			// Identity conflict: a different machine claims an already-registered
   221			// node_id. Reject with 409 and log.
   222			if existingFP != "" && req.IdentityFingerprint != "" && existingFP != req.IdentityFingerprint {
   223				msg := fmt.Sprintf("federation: duplicate node_id %q from different identity (existing=%s, attempted=%s)",
   224					req.NodeID, existingFP, req.IdentityFingerprint)
   225				s.hub.conflictsLog = append(s.hub.conflictsLog, msg)
   226				s.hub.warnLog("%s", msg)
   227				writeJSON(w, http.StatusConflict, map[string]string{
   228					"error":  "node_id already registered to a different identity",
   229					"reason": "duplicate_node_id",
   230				})
   231				return
   232			}
   233		}
   234	
   235		rec := federation.SpokeRecord{
   236			NodeID:        req.NodeID,
   237			Name:          req.Name,
   238			URL:           req.URL,
   239			DDxVersion:    req.DDxVersion,
   240			SchemaVersion: req.SchemaVersion,
   241			Capabilities:  append([]string(nil), req.Capabilities...),
   242			RegisteredAt:  now,
   243			LastHeartbeat: time.Time{},
   244			Status:        hs.Status,
   245		}
   246		setIdentityFingerprint(&rec, req.IdentityFingerprint)
   247		if err := s.hub.registry.UpsertSpoke(rec); err != nil {
   248			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
   249			return
   250		}
   251		s.persistFederationLocked()
   252	
   253		// Emit WARN if this registration was accepted via the plain-HTTP opt-out.
   254		if s.hub.allowPlainHTTP && !isTrusted(r) {
   255			msg := fmt.Sprintf("federation: accepted plain-HTTP registration node_id=%q url=%q from remote=%s",
   256				req.NodeID, req.URL, r.RemoteAddr)
   257			s.hub.warnings = append(s.hub.warnings, msg)
   258			s.hub.warnLog("%s", msg)
   259		}

No commit.

The `internal/federation` dead-wrapper issue is already gone in the current tree: `LoadStateFrom` and `SaveStateTo` are the live APIs in [persistence.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8c273456-20260507T004616-70096312/cli/internal/federation/persistence.go#L40) and are wired from [federation_hub.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8c273456-20260507T004616-70096312/cli/internal/server/federation_hub.go#L51), [federation_hub.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8c273456-20260507T004616-70096312/cli/internal/server/federation_hub.go#L57), and [federation_hub.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8c273456-20260507T004616-70096312/cli/internal/server/federation_hub.go#L342). `deadcode` reported no remaining `internal/federation` symbols.
